evals.prompt: TodayView feels sluggish when I have a lot of tasks — maybe 50+. Can you audit the performance of TodayView and its ViewModel and figure out what's slow?
evals.expected_output: Agent should read TodayViewModel and TodayView, identify potential performance bottlenecks like unnecessary @Published updates, N+1 fetches, or heavy body computations, and suggest fixes
expectations: The agent reads TodayViewModel.swift to analyze data loading patterns
The agent reads TodayView.swift to check for expensive body computations
The agent checks for N+1 Core Data fetch patterns
The agent checks for unnecessary @Published property updates
The agent provides specific, actionable recommendations with code examples
The agent suggests writing a performance test with measure{} blocks

evals.prompt: do a full performance audit of the app — I want to make sure we're not doing anything obviously dumb before the v1.10 release
evals.expected_output: Agent should audit multiple critical paths: startup, main screen rendering, Core Data fetches, calendar sync, and memory usage patterns across key ViewModels and Services
expectations: The agent audits more than just one screen — checks multiple ViewModels and Services
The agent checks for memory retain cycles (missing [weak self] in closures)
The agent checks Core Data fetch patterns across services (fetchBatchSize, predicates)
The agent checks for main thread blocking operations
The agent prioritizes findings by impact (high/medium/low)
The agent provides a structured summary of findings
